body_txt: Stealing, the last time we checked, is still a crime. Yet, some people simply cannot help themselves from doing it, be it from a store, their place of employment, or even a loved one's home. Stealing from someone's front porch is quite common as well, and in spite of the rise of the " porch pirates " phenomenon within the past few decades, is nothing new (swiping someone's newspaper or milk delivery has been happening for ages). Although this is unlikely to go away anytime soon, the increase in sales of doorbell cameras as well as prepared homeowners has at least curtailed the occurrence of it somewhat. Also, while delivered packages seem to be the most popular, other items aren't off-limits, including delivered food, lawn decorations, and the house key under the mat (which is why the latter object has fallen out of practice). Related to Pie on the Windowsill , Ding-Dong-Ditch Distraction , and Burning Bag of Poop for other shenanigans involving someone's front porch/property. Not to be confused with Doorstop Baby (though they may overlap). Also not to be confused with stealing actual porches (hopefully) . If actual pirates show up on people's porches, they may be A Pirate 400 Years Too Late ; if pirates are sitting around on their own porches, that might be The Pirates Who Don't Do Anything . Additionally, keep in mind that this trope is in spirit of the clearly illegal types of theft: if it's more along the lines of something retrieved from the garbage or something left out as garbage, then it shouldn't apply. It's generally agreed that a Dumpster Dive is fair game. No relation to Ruthless Modern Pirates . Because of the commonplace occurrence of this crime and its controversy, real-life examples are unnecessary .
